blocking: RED claim does not hold with the prescribed assertions. The plan (L77-79, step 1 L259-260) asserts the reworked High/Low tests will FAIL against the pre-change path-aware PlanFidelity (genuine RED). But traced against the actual code: the CURRENT High events (Write internal/harness/analyzer.go + Command go) score 2/2=1.0 old, 1/1=1.0 new; the CURRENT Low events (Write cmd/unrelated.go + Command ls) score 0/3=0.0 old, 0/1=0.0 new. The existing threshold assertions (score<0.5 / score>0.5) PASS identically under both old and new scoring. Removing only path terms lowers numerator and denominator proportionally and never flips a High>=0.5 or Low<0.5 verdict, so NO threshold-style rework produces RED. A genuine RED requires an EXACT-score assertion (e.g. High score == 1.0 against a plan whose old function would compute 2/3 from an un-touched mentioned path) -- the plan mentions 'in fact 1.0, matched == total == 1' parenthetically but does not REQUIRE the exact-equality assertion. Fix: mandate exact-score assertions (==1.0 / ==0.0) in the reworked tests, or supply an explicit RED witness plan+events pair that the path-aware function scores differently from the commands-only function, so step-1 'confirm they fail against the pre-change function' is actually achievable.
summary: Removal-as-a-unit is accurate and sound: the five sites (call L544, short-circuit L547, total L551, path-match block L554-569, function L694-716) match the real PlanFidelity body, leave no orphan expectedPaths/touchedPaths ref, and yield an internally-consistent commands-only function (denominator len(expectedCommands), short-circuit returns 1.0 when no commands; filepath stays imported via L251 filepath.Base so no dead-import). Hermetic filtered gate (go test -run TestAnalyzer_PlanFidelity ./internal/harness/) is correctly pinned everywhere and the whole-package run is forbidden. ZFC-clean: dropping the prose extension-guessing path extractor satisfies ADR-0036. BLOCKING: the test-rework RED->GREEN is not genuine -- the existing threshold assertions pass under BOTH old and new scoring, so the rework as prescribed is not a real RED witness; it needs exact-score assertions or an explicit divergent witness pair.
